name: Chewa
description: The Chewa are the dominant ethnic group in eastern Zambia and the majority ethnic group in Malawi (where their language Chichewa is a national language). The Nyau brotherhood — their secret masquerade society — is one of the most sophisticated and well-preserved secret societies in central Africa, recognised on UNESCO's Intangible Cultural Heritage list. The Gule Wamkulu (Great Dance) performed by the Nyau is a complete cosmological performance system connecting the living, the dead, and the spirit world.
cultural_highlights: Nyau secret society — male initiation brotherhood that maintains cosmological knowledge through the Gule Wamkulu masquerade; membership is obligatory for Chewa men
Gule Wamkulu (Great Dance) — UNESCO Intangible Heritage; masked dancers representing ancestral spirits, animals, and supernatural beings appear at initiations and funerals
Chieftaincy system — the Paramount Chief Kalonga Gawa Undi is recognised as the highest Chewa chief across Zambia, Malawi, and Mozambique
Female initiation (Chinamwali) — elaborate female coming-of-age ceremony lasting several days, teaching sexual and social knowledge

food_traditions.name: Nsima (maize ugali)
food_traditions.description: Thick maize flour porridge eaten with relish — virtually identical across Zambia, Malawi, and Mozambique, the pan-Chewa staple

food_traditions.name: Nkhwani (pumpkin leaves)
food_traditions.description: Pumpkin leaf stew with groundnut paste — shared with Bemba and Tonga; a universal Zambian green vegetable

food_traditions.name: Chimanga chophika (roasted maize)
food_traditions.description: Roasted sweetcorn on the cob — the definitive Zambian seasonal street food

music_traditions: Gule Wamkulu dance music — specific drum patterns and songs that 'call' specific masquerade characters into appearance
Vimbuza — a healing dance and trance ceremony where spirit possession is used to diagnose and treat illness
Ingoma ya Nyau — rhythmic drumming ensemble accompanying all Nyau ceremonies
